recipes.title: Bibimbap
recipes.description: Mixed rice bowl with seasoned vegetables, marinated beef, fried egg and gochujang sauce.
steps: Slice beef thinly and marinate with soy sauce, garlic and a teaspoon of sesame oil for 20 minutes.
Blanch spinach, squeeze and season with sesame oil, garlic and salt.
Blanch bean sprouts and dress with sesame oil, garlic and salt.
Saute julienned carrot, zucchini and sliced mushrooms separately with a pinch of salt.
Stir-fry the beef in a hot pan until just cooked.
Fry an egg sunny-side up for each serving.
Pile rice in bowls, arrange vegetables and beef in sections, top with egg and gochujang.

recipes.title: Bulgogi
recipes.description: Thinly sliced beef marinated in sweet soy sauce with pear, garlic and sesame, then grilled.
steps: Slice beef paper-thin against the grain, ideally partially frozen.
Grate pear and onion, mix with soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger and pepper.
Marinate beef with the mixture for at least 2 hours.
Heat a heavy skillet or grill plate until smoking.
Cook beef in single-layer batches for 2 minutes, tossing once.
Finish with sliced scallions and sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Kimchi Jjigae
recipes.description: Hearty Korean stew of aged kimchi, pork belly and tofu in a spicy umami broth.
steps: Cut pork belly into bite-sized pieces and chop kimchi into 3 cm pieces.
Saute pork in a heavy pot until edges crisp.
Add kimchi, gochugaru and gochujang and stir-fry 5 minutes.
Pour in anchovy stock with sliced onion and crushed garlic.
Simmer 25 minutes, then add cubed tofu and cook 5 minutes more.
Finish with scallions and sesame oil.

recipes.title: Tteokbokki
recipes.description: Chewy rice cakes simmered in a sweet and spicy gochujang sauce with fish cakes.
steps: Soak rice cakes in cold water for 20 minutes.
Combine stock with gochujang, gochugaru, sugar, soy sauce and minced garlic in a wide pan.
Bring to a boil and add rice cakes, fish cake slices and shredded cabbage.
Simmer 10 minutes, stirring often, until sauce reduces and thickens.
Add boiled eggs and scallions in the last minute.
Serve hot from the pan.

recipes.title: Japchae
recipes.description: Sweet potato glass noodles stir-fried with beef, mushrooms and rainbow vegetables.
steps: Soak glass noodles in warm water for 30 minutes, then boil for 5 minutes and drain.
Slice beef thinly and marinate with soy sauce, sugar and garlic.
Stir-fry vegetables separately in sesame oil with a pinch of salt to keep colours bright.
Stir-fry beef and mushrooms together until cooked.
Toss noodles in a wok with soy sauce, sugar and remaining sesame oil until glossy.
Combine all components and finish with sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Samgyeopsal
recipes.description: Korean grilled pork belly served with lettuce wraps, ssamjang and garlic.
steps: Slice pork belly into 1 cm thick strips.
Mix doenjang, gochujang, sesame oil and a little water to make ssamjang.
Heat a grill plate at the table or a heavy skillet.
Grill pork belly until both sides are crisp and golden.
Slice into bite-sized pieces with kitchen scissors.
Wrap pork in lettuce with rice, ssamjang, garlic, chilli and kimchi.

recipes.title: Sundubu Jjigae
recipes.description: Bubbling stew of soft silken tofu, seafood and gochugaru in a fiery red broth.
steps: Saute pork belly in sesame oil in an earthenware pot.
Add gochugaru, garlic and chopped onion and stir-fry until fragrant.
Pour in anchovy stock and bring to a boil.
Add clams and shrimp and simmer 4 minutes until clams open.
Spoon in soft tofu in large chunks and season with soy sauce.
Crack eggs into the bubbling stew, top with scallions and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Galbi
recipes.description: Korean-style marinated short ribs grilled until caramelised at the edges.
steps: Soak short ribs in cold water for 30 minutes to remove blood, then drain.
Blend pear, onion, garlic and ginger with soy sauce, sugar, mirin and sesame oil.
Marinate ribs in the mixture for at least 4 hours.
Heat a grill or heavy pan until very hot.
Grill ribs for 3 minutes per side until caramelised.
Garnish with scallions and sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Haemul Pajeon
recipes.description: Crispy Korean seafood and scallion pancake served with a soy-vinegar dipping sauce.
steps: Whisk flours with cold water and egg until smooth.
Trim and halve the scallions, slice squid into rings, chop shrimp.
Heat oil in a wide skillet over medium-high heat.
Lay scallions flat, pour batter over them, top with seafood.
Cook 4 minutes, then flip and press down, cooking 4 more minutes until crisp.
Mix soy sauce, vinegar and gochugaru for dipping.

recipes.title: Dakgalbi
recipes.description: Spicy stir-fried chicken with cabbage, sweet potato and rice cakes in gochujang sauce.
steps: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces.
Mix gochujang, gochugaru, soy sauce, sugar and minced garlic into a marinade.
Combine chicken with marinade and rest 30 minutes.
Heat a wide skillet with oil over medium-high heat.
Add chicken with sliced sweet potato, cabbage and onion.
Stir-fry 12 minutes until chicken is cooked and cabbage softens.
Add rice cakes, cook 5 more minutes and finish with shredded perilla.

recipes.title: Naengmyeon
recipes.description: Cold Korean buckwheat noodles in an icy beef broth with cucumber, pear and boiled egg.
steps: Simmer brisket with onion in water for 90 minutes, season with salt and chill broth overnight.
Skim solidified fat and stir vinegar and sugar into the broth, then freeze partially to slush.
Cook naengmyeon noodles for 3 minutes and rinse thoroughly under cold water.
Slice cucumber and pear into matchsticks.
Hard-boil and halve the eggs.
Pile noodles in chilled bowls, top with brisket, cucumber, pear and egg, then pour over icy broth.
Serve with mustard paste on the side.

recipes.title: Kimbap
recipes.description: Korean seaweed rice rolls filled with vegetables, egg and seasoned beef or fish.
steps: Season warm rice with sesame oil, salt and sesame seeds.
Julienne and stir-fry the carrots until tender, then season lightly.
Blanch and squeeze spinach, dress with sesame oil and salt.
Beat eggs, cook into a thin omelette and slice into strips.
On a bamboo mat with nori, spread a thin layer of rice leaving a border.
Lay vegetable, egg and ham strips across the centre and roll tightly.
Brush roll with sesame oil and slice into 1.5 cm pieces.

recipes.title: Yangnyeom Chicken
recipes.description: Korean fried chicken double-fried for crispness then tossed in a sweet-spicy gochujang glaze.
steps: Pat wings dry and toss with salt, pepper and potato starch.
Heat oil to 160C and fry wings 8 minutes, then drain.
Reheat oil to 190C and fry wings 5 minutes until deep golden, then drain.
Simmer gochujang, ketchup, honey, soy sauce and minced garlic until syrupy.
Toss wings in the warm sauce until evenly coated.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Doenjang Jjigae
recipes.description: Rustic fermented soybean paste stew with zucchini, tofu, mushrooms and anchovy stock.
steps: Cube beef and saute in an earthenware pot until browned.
Add gochugaru and minced garlic and stir 30 seconds.
Pour in anchovy stock and whisk in doenjang.
Add diced potato, onion and zucchini and simmer 10 minutes.
Add cubed tofu and sliced mushrooms and cook 5 more minutes.
Top with scallions and serve bubbling hot.

recipes.title: Hotteok
recipes.description: Korean street pancakes with chewy yeast dough and a melted brown sugar cinnamon walnut filling.
steps: Mix yeast and sugar with warm water and rest 10 minutes until foamy.
Combine flours and salt, add yeast mixture and knead into a sticky dough.
Cover and rise in a warm place for 1 hour until doubled.
Mix brown sugar, cinnamon and chopped walnuts for the filling.
Oil hands and divide dough into 8 pieces, flatten and stuff with filling, sealing edges.
Pan-fry in oil over medium heat, pressing flat with a spatula, 2 minutes per side.

recipes.title: Mandu
recipes.description: Korean dumplings filled with pork, tofu and kimchi, steamed or pan-fried.
steps: Squeeze tofu and chopped kimchi to remove excess liquid.
Mix pork, tofu, kimchi, finely chopped onion, scallion, garlic, soy sauce, sesame oil and pepper.
Place a teaspoon of filling on each wrapper, wet edges and pleat into half-moons.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan and lay mandu flat-side down.
Fry 2 minutes until golden, add 60 ml water and cover.
Steam 6 minutes until water evaporates and bottoms are crisp.
